{"step_by_step_thinking": "The upper lip develops from embryological fusion of specific facial processes. According to embryology, the upper lip is formed by the fusion of the maxillary processes and the medial (median) nasal processes. The lateral nasal processes contribute to the nasal alae, not the upper lip. The mandibular process does not contribute to the upper lip formation. Document [30] explicitly states: 'During the sixth to seventh weeks of embryonic development, the bilateral maxillary prominences grow medially towards the medial nasal prominences, and fusion occurs, leading to the formation of the upper lip. The fusion of the bilateral medial nasal prominences forms the medial portion of the upper lip, whereas the paired maxillary prominences form the lateral aspects of the upper lip.' This confirms that the upper lip results from the fusion of the maxillary and median (medial) nasal processes. The lateral nasal process does not contribute to the upper lip.
